L1.area: Cryptographic signature serialization and deserialization logic

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the DER-encoded signature parsing logic of an elliptic curve cryptography library. The length decoding routine for DER structures does not properly bound or validate multi-byte length values, allowing integer overflow through bit-shifting operations. Additionally, the parsing routine accepts non-canonical encodings including leading zero bytes and overlong length representations, enabling signature malleability. An attacker could craft signatures with modified DER encoding that are still accepted as valid, undermining signature uniqueness guarantees.

L3.area: ECDSA signature DER encoding and decoding within the elliptic curve module
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the DER signature parsing logic of an ECDSA implementation. The multi-byte length decoding does not limit the number of length octets or apply unsigned integer conversion, allowing bit-shift operations to overflow. Additionally, non-canonical DER encodings such as leading zero bytes in the R and S integer components and overlong length representations are not rejected, enabling signature malleability. An attacker can craft DER-encoded ECDSA signatures with modified encoding that pass verification, violating the expectation of canonical signature representation.

ground_truth.reason: The getLength function in the DER signature parser allows integer overflow via unbounded bit-shifting of multi-byte length octets, and the _importDER function accepts non-canonical DER encodings with leading zeros, enabling ECDSA signature malleability.
